positive.comment: Really cool. Maybe as an upsell, you could allow users to "gift a response" as part of their letter. In other words, prepaying for their recipient's response. The letter could come with a tiny tag or something where the recipient would essentially be able to scan a QR code or go to a custom link that lets them send a letter back to the original sender at no cost to them. Added benefit of increasing user adoption.

Just some thoughts to help the flow. - !!! Nice subtle gradient animation in the background. - The header logo can be clicked on within the main-page and I get a little nod and a wink, but visiting other pages like your About, Privacy, Terms does not show the animation and does provide a link back to the Main Page. So you might add a "Call to Action" / 'Send a letter' button in a top corner of your header so visitors are always brought back to the main page. - Lastly, I think the navigation on the bottom of your site could be placed above your page header below your little hat icon...

hi OP, i really like your project so far but i have some suggestions: - allow other payment methods such as paypal. some people are very conscious about sharing their card details and are not willing to easily share it, but are willing to use paypal or something else - are you going to review the content of the letters? - i haven't tried sending a letter via your service yet but i hope there are other choices for the stamp - an app would be neat as well! - while i don't mind the font that you use, it would be neat if there are choices for font styles as well - i wish there was an option to add more files or to resize the images

I do not know if it's country specific thing but in Bulgaria when you send letters, the sender names and addresses are in top left corner and the recipient is in the bottom right, stamp is on top right of the envelope. Additionally the name of the sender and address is put in the first sheet of the letter itself again in the top left corner. One more suggestion, you can experiment with handwriting fonts customisations... Map page is a bit hard to navigate and to check which countries are filled. Maybe will be good addition to add country list and count of sent letters. Good luck with it!

The address doesn't adapt to regional address standards. While in Belgium and the Netherlands, the building number follows the street, in North America, the custom is that the number precedes the street in the first line of the address. This might cause some small confusion and would help if remedied.
Your addresses in the UK are putting the number at the end of the street name which is not typical for letters here, it should be at the start.

Nice, just ordered a letter to myself. One thing is it lets you enter an address without some fields (eg city) but this throws an error when you go to pay, and the error message wasn't very clear about the problem, had to try 3 times to work it out.

negative.comment: Aw man. Well I'd demand proof of it being sent out or else a refund, their costumer service was really sketchy and the no tracking is also pretty weird :/ even though initially on their website it said there's international tracking, when I contacted their support about it they said it was "lost in translation". The person who built this website has built another site just for the money, a "pay to message" site. Starting to think that's how they profit, by making people believe the "cheap 3£ letters" are worth it since some of us can't send it out and this service does it for you...and then boom, collects 3£ from thousands of people, easy money. Idk what I expected but I'm not surprised, this is the third letter service I've seen scamming. The pictures look fake too.
